reasoning: The current price as of 10/24/2025 is 909.36, following a steep ~8% plunge from ~987 following a Q3 earnings miss on EPS (where revenue actually slightly beat and full-year guidance was maintained). The first resolution date (Nov 2) resolves on Friday, Oct 31, which is just five trading days away. Right after an earnings miss, stocks can either see negative momentum drift or an oversold rebound. Since the stock essentially closed flat on the second day after the drop (from 910.33 to 909.36), it appears to have found an initial stabilization point. However, over purely a 5-day horizon, momentum is still risky, so probability stays right near 0.50 (slightly biased lower due to post-earnings drag/uncertainty). Over the medium to long term (Nov 25 and Jan 24), the fundamental narrative takes over. URI maintained solid guidance, has no immediate debt maturities, and top analysts (e.g., KeyBanc) reaffirmed an Overweight rating maintaining a target of $1,120, well above current prices. The broader context of market upward drift (beta) and mean reversion for a fundamentally sound company makes it increasingly likely that the stock will recover and rise back above 909.4 as the time horizons extend. Therefore, probabilities scale up to 0.52 for late November and 0.55 for January 2026.
